title: Disability Awareness Training - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Disability Awareness Training 7 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARC UMATILLA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides connections to resources such as case management services, assistance with advocacy, disability awareness training, education, recreational programs, and vocational training. 215 W Orchard Avenue Hermiston, OR 978381738 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families Hours: Monday 9am-noon Email (541) 567-7615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST ADA CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information, training, and guidance on the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), including fact sheets addressing commonly asked questions. 1701 NE Columbia Road Seattle, WA 98195 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (800) 949-4232 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE EDUCATION AND TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides training on topics such as collaborative problem solving, mental health first aid, self-care, trauma-informed care, and family-led crisis planning. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Professionals, volunteers, staff, community partners, youth, mentors, peer support, and family members Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 709-1366 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC UMATILLA COUNTY ARC UMATILLA COUNTY CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK EDUCATION AND TRAINING OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER
